module_map.responsibility: HTTP request/response abstraction layer. Provides HttpRequest (with GET, POST, COOKIES, META, FILES attributes) and HttpResponse classes for all request/response handling.
URL routing and pattern matching. Resolves HTTP request paths to view functions/classes. Maintains URL configuration and reverse URL resolution.
Request/response pipeline orchestration. Implements BaseHandler, WSGIHandler, and ASGIHandler that coordinate middleware execution, URL resolution, and view dispatch.

Request/response interceptors that implement cross-cutting concerns: CommonMiddleware, CsrfMiddleware, CacheMiddleware, SecurityMiddleware, etc. Each extends MiddlewareMixin with process_request/response hooks.
Request handler functions and View classes. Entry point for business logic after URL dispatch. Includes base View, generic views, and default error views.
ORM layer and database abstraction. Models, querysets, migrations. Core of data persistence.
Template rendering engine. Processes template files with context data and produces HTML/text output.
Form handling and validation. Converts HTML forms to Python objects, validates input, and generates form HTML.
Signal/event dispatcher system. Decoupled publish-subscribe pattern for app communication (pre_save, post_save, request_started, etc.).
Configuration management. Loads Django settings, provides settings object, and locale/format configuration per language.
App registry and configuration. Manages installed apps, app metadata, and ready signal for app initialization.
Core utilities: signals, exceptions, caching, mail, validation, pagination. Cross-cutting infrastructure.
General utilities: encoding, data structures, functional helpers, regex caching, logging, module loading.

pipeline_trace.description: WSGI server calls WSGIHandler.__call__ with environ dict. Signals request_started. Creates WSGIRequest by wrapping environ.
BaseHandler.load_middleware constructs middleware chain. Each middleware wraps the next handler. Processes process_request hooks in order. CommonMiddleware validates user agent, handles redirects.
BaseHandler.get_response calls get_resolver(settings.ROOT_URLCONF).resolve(request.path_info). URLResolver matches path against patterns, returns ResolverMatch with view func and kwargs.
Matched view callable invoked with request and kwargs. If class-based view, dispatch() routes to get/post/put/delete. View calls model querysets or ORM operations.
View calls Model.objects.all() or QuerySet methods. QuerySet builds Query object, backend connection executes SQL, returns model instances with cached results.
View renders context with template. Template engine processes template_name or content, substitutes variables, calls template tags. Returns rendered string.
BaseHandler calls process_response hooks in reverse order. Middleware can modify response headers, compress, set cache headers. Handles 404 redirects.
WSGIHandler.__call__ returns response iterator to WSGI server. Emits request_finished signal. Server serializes HTTP status, headers, body to client.

cross_module_hubs.reason: Settings object is central to all modules: middleware, handlers, urls, db, apps, core. Every component reads settings.DEBUG, settings.MIDDLEWARE, settings.ROOT_URLCONF, etc. No outbound deps because conf is leaf.

BaseHandler orchestrates the entire request lifecycle: imports and coordinates middleware, url resolution, signal emission, db transactions, exception handling, logging. Central coordinator of pipeline.

Utility functions imported everywhere: cached_property in models/http/forms, MultiValueDict in http/querysets, encoding across all modules. Generic utilities support higher-level modules without depending on them.

change_proposal.feature_summary: Add async-compatible structured logging middleware that captures per-request metadata (HTTP method, path, matched view name, ORM query count, response time, status code) and emits as structured JSON log entries via Python logging.
new_types: RequestMetadata (dataclass holding method, path, view_name, query_count, response_time, status_code)
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: Follow CommonMiddleware and CsrfMiddleware patterns: inherit MiddlewareMixin, implement process_request() and process_response() hooks. Use request attributes (resolver_match, META) for metadata capture. Support both sync/async via sync_capable=True, async_capable=True.
change_proposal.integration_point: Middleware stack in core/handlers/base.py BaseHandler.load_middleware(). Must run early (after security/auth middleware but before view execution) to capture query counts. Attach RequestMetadata to request object during process_request. Log in process_response with timing info from request start time.
risks: Performance overhead: structured logging adds JSON serialization per request; mitigated by conditional logging (DEBUG=False in production, or log_level filtering).
Query counting complexity: must hook db.backends.utils.CursorWrapper or use django.db.connection.queries (DEBUG=True only); risk of missing or double-counting async queries.
Async/sync compatibility: must handle both sync_to_async and async_to_sync adapted handlers; testing required for async views and middleware chains.
Circular import risk: RequestMetadata and StructuredFormatter in separate module to avoid importing middleware in core.handlers.
Data sensitivity: query strings and request bodies may contain PII; middleware should have config to redact or exclude sensitive paths.

tool_usage.params: ls to discover module structure at /tmp/benchmark-repos/django/django/
Read django/middleware/common.py to understand middleware pattern (MiddlewareMixin, process_request, process_response)
Read django/http/request.py (lines 1-100) to understand HttpRequest attributes and structure
Read django/http/response.py (lines 1-80) to understand HttpResponse and ResponseHeaders
Read django/urls/resolvers.py (lines 1-100) to understand ResolverMatch and URL resolution flow
Read django/core/handlers/wsgi.py (full) to understand WSGIRequest and WSGIHandler entry point
Read django/core/handlers/base.py (lines 1-150) to understand middleware chain, load_middleware, get_response
ls to enumerate middleware, db models, core handlers, dispatch, and utils subdirectories
head to preview dispatcher.py Signal class structure for understanding signal/dispatch pattern
ls to enumerate utils module utilities available for middleware implementation
